name: Villava - Malkaitz – circular por Ardanatz y el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca de Pamplona
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villava/Atarrabia offers diverse terrain, characterized by the varied landscapes of the Navarra region. The area features imposing mountains, significant rivers like the Arga, and extensive forests, providing a rich backdrop for outdoor activities. Riders can explore scenic routes with views of rural landscapes, historic medieval bridges, and the foothills of the Pyrenees. Peaks such as San Cristóbal Fort on Mount Ezkaba offer challenging climbs and panoramic vistas. Best mountain bike trails around Villava/Atarrabia The most popular mountain bike trail is Villava - Malkaitz – circular por Ardanatz y el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca de Pamplona, a 19.4 miles (31.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Arga River area and surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tangorri Summit – Zokorena Dam loop from Villava / Atarrabia, a moderate 22.6 miles (36.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain towards Tangorri Summit, offering expansive views. Local mountain bikers also love the Zokorena Dam – Irotz Repeater loop from Burlada / Burlata, a 14.7 miles (23.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with climbs, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Mountain biking around Villava/Atarrabia is defined by diverse terrain, Pyrenean foothills, and riverine areas along the Arga River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Villava/Atarrabia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 4000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villava/Atarrabia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villava/Atarrabia?
A: There are over 250 mountain bike trails around Villava/Atarrabia, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy paths to challenging climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Villava/Atarrabia?
A: The terrain around Villava/Atarrabia is highly diverse, characteristic of the Navarra region. You can expect imposing mountains, extensive forests, and riverine areas along the Arga River. Many routes feature scenic views of rural landscapes, historic medieval bridges, and the foothills of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Villava/Atarrabia?
A: Yes, Villava/Atarrabia offers a good selection of trails suitable for beginners and families. There are nearly 50 easy routes available, providing gentle paths to enjoy the scenery without excessive technical difficulty. These routes are perfect for those looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails near Villava/Atarrabia?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are over 50 difficult mountain bike trails. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988231418/villava-malkaitz-circular-por-ardanatz-y-el-parque-fluvial-de-la-comarca-de-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villava - Malkaitz – circular por Ardanatz y el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca de Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;, a 19.4-mile (31.2 km) trail with significant elevation gain exploring the Arga River area and surrounding hills.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Villava/Atarrabia?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31897106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tangorri Summit – Zokorena Dam loop from Villava / Atarrabia&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 22.6-mile (36.4 km) path featuring expansive views from Tangorri Summit.

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer scenic views and pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter historic medieval bridges, or enjoy panoramic vistas from peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2963302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tangorri Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2844637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cristóbal Fort on Mount Ezkaba&lt;/a&gt;, which offers both historical significance and diverse paths to its summit.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Villava/Atarrabia?
A: The diverse landscapes of Navarra make Villava/Atarrabia suitable for mountain biking across various seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While specific seasonal considerations can vary, the region's varied terrain means there are usually suitable routes available year-round, depending on weather conditions.

Q: Are there services for cyclists, such as bike rentals or accommodations, near Villava/Atarrabia?
A: Yes, the Navarra region, including areas around Villava/Atarrabia, provides a network of businesses dedicated to cycle tourism. This includes bike-friendly accommodations, organized cycling tours, and bicycle rental services. For more information on cycling services in Navarra, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/bike-and-mtb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Villava/Atarrabia?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villava/Atarrabia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 500 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views of the Pyrenean foothills and rural landscapes, and the variety of routes catering to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that explore riverine areas?
A: Yes, the area is close to the Arga River, and several trails incorporate its natural environment. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988231418/villava-malkaitz-circular-por-ardanatz-y-el-parque-fluvial-de-la-comarca-de-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villava - Malkaitz – circular por Ardanatz y el Parque Fluvial de la Comarca de Pamplona&lt;/a&gt; route explores the Arga River area and surrounding hills.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a good workout?
A: Absolutely. The region features imposing mountains and peaks that offer challenging climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31897106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tangorri Summit – Zokorena Dam loop from Villava / Atarrabia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with significant elevation gain, providing a rewarding workout and expansive views from the summit.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes offer glimpses of historical significance. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2844637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cristóbal Fort on Mount Ezkaba&lt;/a&gt; is a notable historical site that can be reached via various paths, offering both a physical challenge and a cultural point of interest.
tips: Very nice panoramic views towards the Ezkotitz pond. This viewpoint coincides with the top of the Bordalarre hill at 573 meters above sea level.
To go down, it is advisable to return along the track that goes up from Ardanaz.

Trialera to go down the Malkaitz crest with a lot of unevenness.
Technically difficult and with some exposed dangerous step.
Very cool and entertaining path with beautiful views, especially on the descent from the top of San Miguel towards the valley of the Arga river.

Picturesque path that runs along the river Arga allowing you to appreciate the landscape that surrounds it as you enter the city of Pamplona. There are several places to take a break along the way, be careful that it is a path shared with pedestrians.

Beautiful place on the bed of the Arga river at its confluence with the Urbi river. On one side of it there is a recreational area that is usually a very crowded place in the summer months to take a refreshing bath and the jump from the dam is a natural slide that gives it a touch of entertainment.

Beautiful views from the top of Miravalles where the castle ruins are located. There are several alternative paths to get here, choose according to the technical difficulty, all of them will have very nice views.

Over the bed of the Ultzama River is this picturesque bridge that dates back to the 13th century and was built with the purpose of crossing the river, joining the old Roman Road and later the Camino de Santiago.
